heroHeadline: Trophy Tour Hits Calgary, Iran Silent Day 4 — FIFA Refuses Relocation, Congress in Vancouver in 18 Days, 60 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: The original FIFA World Cup Trophy arrived at Calgary's Scotiabank Saddledome on April 12 as the second stop of the Trophy Tour by Coca-Cola's Canadian leg, following its debut with Fabio Cannavaro at the Vancouver Art Gallery on April 9. Meanwhile, four days after the April 8 Pakistan-brokered US-Iran ceasefire, Iran's government has still issued no revised statement on World Cup participation — even as FIFA formally confirmed on April 10 that Iran's Group G matches will NOT be relocated from US soil. Italy's Sports Minister Andrea Abodi called replacement 'very difficult,' noting any substitute would come from the Asian Football Confederation. The FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 — now 18 days away — remains the formal decision point on Iran's fate. Iran's Group G schedule (SoFi Stadium, LA: June 15 vs. New Zealand, June 21 vs. Belgium; Lumen Field, Seattle: June 26 vs. Egypt) stands unchanged, with the Kino Sports Complex in Tucson confirmed as Iran's training base and 60 days to the June 11 opener.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, Inside World Football
